skill.name: clinical-reports
skill.description: Draft clinical reports, diagnostic documentation, clinical trial reports, and patient notes using structured templates, regulatory references, and local validation utilities.
skill.summary: Create structured clinical documentation with privacy, reporting, and compliance checks.
skill.author: K-Dense-AI

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported 569 potential issues, but sampled context shows the weak crypto, reconnaissance, credential, and Ruby backtick alerts are mainly clinical terminology, Markdown formatting, and reference links. No prompt injection, subprocess execution, network client, environment secret access, or malicious exfiltration intent was found. Risk remains medium because the skill is a community healthcare skill with Bash permission and local scripts that read and write clinical documents that may contain PHI.

medium_findings.title: Local PHI File Processing
medium_findings.description: The included scripts read user-supplied clinical reports and can write JSON or Markdown outputs. This is legitimate validation behavior, but users may process protected health information, so outputs must remain in approved local storage.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file reads and writes are explicit and operate on clinical documents. I did not find network transfer or credential access, so the concern is data handling rather than malicious behavior.

medium_findings.title: Broad Tool Permission With Bash Guidance
medium_findings.description: The skill declares Bash, Write, and Edit permissions and includes a documented bash command for figure generation. The command appears instructional, but broad permissions increase review requirements for a community healthcare skill.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The permission declaration and command block are direct evidence. I did not find subprocess calls in the Python scripts, so legitimate use is likely.

low_findings.title: Static Alerts Are Mostly Documentation False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography, reconnaissance, credential, and Ruby backtick detections are explained by clinical abbreviations, HIPAA terminology, Markdown code formatting, and reference URLs. No evidence found of cryptographic implementation misuse, system probing, or credential collection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Targeted review confirmed the flagged patterns occur in documentation and local utility context. The finding is retained as low because the static analyzer produced many alerts that marketplace reviewers may want summarized.

content.value_statement: Clinical teams need accurate reports that meet privacy and regulatory standards. This skill provides templates and local checks for clinical, diagnostic, trial, and patient documentation.
actual_capabilities: Drafts case reports using CARE guideline sections and publication requirements.
Structures radiology, pathology, and laboratory reports with standard clinical headings.
Prepares clinical trial documents such as SAE reports and ICH-E3 study report outlines.
Creates patient documentation templates, including SOAP notes, H&P reports, consultations, and discharge summaries.
Runs local checks for de-identification, terminology, report completeness, and compliance markers.
Provides reference material for HIPAA, FDA, ICH-GCP, ACR, CAP, and peer review expectations.
limitations: It does not provide diagnosis, treatment recommendations, or clinical decision support.
It cannot verify facts against electronic health records or external patient systems.
Generated reports require clinician, privacy, and institutional review before use.
Local scripts use pattern checks and may miss context-specific privacy or terminology issues.

use_cases.title: Prepare a publishable case report
use_cases.description: Create a CARE-aligned report with timeline, consent, de-identification, and discussion sections.

use_cases.title: Draft trial safety documentation
use_cases.description: Build SAE narratives, adverse event summaries, and clinical study report sections for review.

use_cases.title: Standardize patient documentation
use_cases.description: Generate SOAP notes, H&P documents, consultation notes, and discharge summaries from supplied clinical facts.

prompt_templates.title: Create a SOAP note
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner task for routine patient documentation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a SOAP note from these de-identified encounter details: [paste details]. Keep assessment and plan separate.

prompt_templates.title: Draft a case report outline
prompt_templates.prompt: Draft a CARE-guideline case report outline for this de-identified case: [paste case]. Include missing information questions.

prompt_templates.title: Check clinical privacy risks
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate task before sharing or publication.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this clinical document for HIPAA de-identification issues: [paste text]. List identifiers to remove and explain each risk.

prompt_templates.title: Build a clinical trial report section
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced task for regulated trial documentation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Draft the safety evaluation section of an ICH-E3 clinical study report from these de-identified tables and narratives: [paste content].

output_examples.input: A de-identified case summary for a rare medication reaction.
output_examples.output: A structured case report draft with title, abstract, patient information, timeline, diagnostic assessment, intervention, outcome, discussion, consent statement, and privacy checklist.

output_examples.input: A serious adverse event narrative from a clinical trial site.
output_examples.output: A regulator-ready SAE report section with event chronology, severity, seriousness criteria, causality assessment, expectedness, action taken, outcome, and follow-up needs.

output_examples.input: A draft discharge summary with medications and follow-up instructions.
output_examples.output: A revised discharge summary with organized hospital course, active diagnoses, medication reconciliation, pending results, patient instructions, and de-identification warnings.

best_practices: Use de-identified source material whenever possible and keep PHI inside approved systems.
Have qualified clinicians verify all facts, conclusions, medication details, and regulatory claims.
Run privacy and completeness checks before sharing reports with journals, sponsors, or external reviewers.
anti_patterns: Do not paste identifiable patient data unless your environment is approved for PHI.
Do not treat generated text as medical advice or a final clinical decision.
Do not submit reports without human review for consent, privacy, accuracy, and local policy.

faq.question: What clinical report types are supported?
faq.answer: It supports case reports, radiology reports, pathology reports, lab reports, trial reports, SOAP notes, H&P notes, consultations, and discharge summaries.

faq.question: Can it check HIPAA de-identification?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes local pattern checks for common HIPAA identifiers, but a privacy expert should review final documents.

faq.question: Does it connect to patient records?
faq.answer: No. The skill works from user-provided text and local files. It does not connect to EHR systems.

faq.question: Can clinicians use the output directly?
faq.answer: No. Clinicians must verify accuracy, context, and institutional requirements before clinical or regulatory use.

faq.question: Which standards are referenced?
faq.answer: The resources reference CARE, HIPAA, FDA, ICH-GCP, ICH-E3, ACR, CAP, CONSORT, and peer review expectations.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: The code appears legitimate, but the skill handles sensitive clinical text and includes local file writing and Bash permission.
